title_original: From Training to Simulation to Real-Time Feedback: A GPU Tour of Infleqtion’s Q4Bio Project Toward Quantum-Enabled Biomarker Discovery | Infleqtion
summary_original: Infleqtion, in partnership with the University of Chicago and the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, has been awarded a two million dollar Phase III contract from Wellcome Leap's Q4Bio program to advance quantum-enabled biomarker discovery for cancer. Their platform integrates Infleqtion’s Sqale neutral atom quantum computer with Graphics Processing Unit acceleration, using NVIDIA CUDA-Q and leveraging up to 24,000 NVIDIA A100 Graphics Processing Unit node hours at the Perlmutter supercomputer. They employ GPU-accelerated optimization to train Instantaneous Quantum Polynomial models with up to twelve logical qubits, validated through GPU simulations before quantum inference. This workflow, which includes real-time control and feedback innovations, enables detection of complex correlations in large-scale biomedical data previously inaccessible using conventional methods.
